Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a normalized order from the ingested Excel files.

importBatchId.description: Reference to the ImportBatch that this order belongs to. (Relationship: ImportBatch 1:N Order)

causaRaizId.description: Reference to the RootCause. (Relationship: RootCause 1:N Order)

conceptoNormalizado.description: Normalized concept for the order. Populated in Stage 4.

impactoNeto.description: Net impact of the order (aditiva - deductiva).

qcFlags.description: Quality control flags for the order.

ImportBatch.title: ImportBatch
ImportBatch.description: Represents a batch of imported data from an Excel file.

id.description: Unique identifier for the import batch.

fileName.description: Name of the imported Excel file.

storageReference.description: Reference to the file in cloud storage.

importDate.description: Date and time of the import.

RootCause.title: RootCause
RootCause.description: Represents a root cause from a catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the root cause.

definition.description: Stores normalized order data ingested from Excel files.
Tracks batches of imported data from Excel files.

params.name: importBatchId
params.description: Unique identifier for the import batch.

definition.description: Stores a catalog of root causes.

params.name: rootCauseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the root cause.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the ingestion, normalization, and analysis of order data from Excel files, focusing on authorization independence, clear segregation of data, and predictable schema. It incorporates `orders`, `projects`, `importBatches`, and `rootCauses` collections. The structure emphasizes denormalization for authorization independence and segregation of data for security. Specifically, the `orders` collection holds the normalized order data, linking to `importBatches` and `rootCauses`. `projects` collection stores project metadata. `importBatches` tracks the ingested Excel files, and `rootCauses` contains a catalog of root causes. This structure ensures data integrity, facilitates efficient queries, and simplifies security rules.

**Authorization Independence:** The design achieves authorization independence by avoiding hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()`). Security rules can be written independently for each collection without needing to retrieve data from parent documents.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   List operations are secured by path-based access control, ensuring that users can only access data they are authorized to view.
*   The segregation of `orders`, `projects`, `importBatches`, and `rootCauses` into separate collections allows for specific security rules tailored to each type of data, preventing unauthorized access.
*   The structure allows secure `list` operations and avoids filtering on the client-side, aligning with the principle that "Rules are not Filters".
